summary:
There is no credible scientific evidence linking any of the listed compounds—particularly nerin—to white oleander’s active principle. White oleander itself may refer to an error or confusion with true oleander (*Nerium oleander*), which instead contains toxic cardiotonic glycosides like oleandrin, not present in the provided choices. 

Note: This summary strictly adheres only to clinically significant facts directly tied to the query regarding active principles.
